title: 2026 01 11 Rankett Meetup Prep Revised
html:
TL;DR

  • Second-pass rewrite of the Rankett GEO/AI Visibility meetup briefing (4 days after the Jan 7 cut) — same 50 Q&A spine, but tighter prose and a refreshed Three-User Model + Pooled Data Advantage sections.
  • Re-asserts the "prompts are the new keywords" tagline and reframes the AI visibility engine as visibility across chatgpt, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, google ai overviews, Grok, and DeepSeek — the seven-platform analysis spread.
  • Doc tail captures live cofounder feedback being processed in real time: an agency told Yahir the platform's competitor list was subpar for global-scope clients — Yahir asks for "genuine independent analysis" rather than a yes-man take.
  • Surfaces the partner tier structure (Tier 1: 1–4 clients, Tier 2: 5–9, Tier 3: 10+) as the support-allocation mechanism that lets Rankett scale without per-agency custom work.
  • The closing prompts ("orient me on what remains for our tool and website" + "what does closing the gap look like") show this is also a founder briefing for himself — using the meetup as a forcing function to reconcile the cofounder backlog.

Core content

The doc opens with a 10-section deep dive that reads as Yahir's revised mental model post-meetup-prep — most useful section is the Evolution from Old Model to New: Rankett killed direct-client work, ad-hoc fulfillment, and mixed offers in favor of one avatar, one offer, one funnel, standardized fulfillment. This is the Rankett reset moment captured in writing.

The Pooled Data Advantage gets its first formal articulation here: every new partner can sell with proof generated by older partners. Day-one credibility without 90 days of waiting. This is the pooled data network effect that the partner program doc only implied.

The 50 Q&A is structured for Diddles (or any future sales-side hire) to absorb in one sitting and have a confident answer for any objection — "I can build this myself", "AI search isn't big yet", "What if it doesn't work?". The objection-handler table at the end is meetup-floor ammo.

Tail contains real product-feedback from a Rankett pilot agency: their competitor-suggestion engine isn't great for non-local scope. Yahir's response — "what is your genuine independent analysis of this take" — is methodologically interesting. He's actively building the muscle of asking AI for adversarial review rather than confirmation.

Stub-promotion notes

  • grok, google ai overviews — STUB (entities, AI platforms in the seven-platform set)
  • pooled data network effect — already exists; reaches 4+ mentions, fully promoted

Connections

  • rankett — parent project
  • 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide — first cut (4 days earlier)
  • 2026 01 05 rankett partner program — infrastructure layer
  • ai search optimization — category
  • asking the right question — methodological echo in the cofounder-feedback section

related.label: Rankett Meetup Prep Guide — GTM Bible
